verses.text: Asaph affirms God is good to the pure in heart, yet confesses his feet nearly slipped, for he 'envied the foolish' when he saw the wicked prosper. Their ease unsettled his faith. The success of the godless has tripped many a believer.

Asaph affirms God is good to the pure in heart, yet confesses his feet nearly slipped, for he 'envied the foolish' when he saw the wicked prosper. Their ease unsettled his faith. The success of the godless has tripped many a believer.

Asaph affirms God is good to the pure in heart, yet confesses his feet nearly slipped, for he 'envied the foolish' when he saw the wicked prosper. Their ease unsettled his faith. The success of the godless has tripped many a believer.
They seem to have no struggles, untroubled and strong, wearing pride like a chain and violence like a robe. Their life looks charmed. Outward ease can mask a heart far from God.
They seem to have no struggles, untroubled and strong, wearing pride like a chain and violence like a robe. Their life looks charmed. Outward ease can mask a heart far from God.
They seem to have no struggles, untroubled and strong, wearing pride like a chain and violence like a robe. Their life looks charmed. Outward ease can mask a heart far from God.
Fat with prosperity, they speak loftily, setting their mouth 'against the heavens.' Wealth breeds arrogance. The comfortable wicked grow bold to blaspheme the God who feeds them.
Fat with prosperity, they speak loftily, setting their mouth 'against the heavens.' Wealth breeds arrogance. The comfortable wicked grow bold to blaspheme the God who feeds them.
Fat with prosperity, they speak loftily, setting their mouth 'against the heavens.' Wealth breeds arrogance. The comfortable wicked grow bold to blaspheme the God who feeds them.
People flock to them; they prosper in the world and increase in riches, seeming to defy God with impunity. Their case bewilders the faithful. Why the ungodly thrive is an ancient and painful riddle.
People flock to them; they prosper in the world and increase in riches, seeming to defy God with impunity. Their case bewilders the faithful. Why the ungodly thrive is an ancient and painful riddle.
People flock to them; they prosper in the world and increase in riches, seeming to defy God with impunity. Their case bewilders the faithful. Why the ungodly thrive is an ancient and painful riddle.
Asaph almost concludes he has 'cleansed my heart in vain,' being plagued daily while they go free. Doubt presses hard. The tried believer may wonder if godliness is worth its cost.
Asaph almost concludes he has 'cleansed my heart in vain,' being plagued daily while they go free. Doubt presses hard. The tried believer may wonder if godliness is worth its cost.

He would not voice it, lest he wrong God's children, and it troubled him 'until I went into the sanctuary of God; then understood I their end.' Worship cleared his sight. In God's presence the riddle finds its answer.

He would not voice it, lest he wrong God's children, and it troubled him 'until I went into the sanctuary of God; then understood I their end.' Worship cleared his sight. In God's presence the riddle finds its answer.

He would not voice it, lest he wrong God's children, and it troubled him 'until I went into the sanctuary of God; then understood I their end.' Worship cleared his sight. In God's presence the riddle finds its answer.
He sees the wicked set 'in slippery places,' brought to ruin in a moment, their prosperity a fading dream. Their footing is sand. What looked like blessing was the edge of a precipice.
He sees the wicked set 'in slippery places,' brought to ruin in a moment, their prosperity a fading dream. Their footing is sand. What looked like blessing was the edge of a precipice.
He sees the wicked set 'in slippery places,' brought to ruin in a moment, their prosperity a fading dream. Their footing is sand. What looked like blessing was the edge of a precipice.

Ashamed of his bitterness, he owns God still holds his hand, will 'guide me with thy counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.' Faith returns and rises. The believer's poor portion now ends in everlasting glory.

Ashamed of his bitterness, he owns God still holds his hand, will 'guide me with thy counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.' Faith returns and rises. The believer's poor portion now ends in everlasting glory.

Ashamed of his bitterness, he owns God still holds his hand, will 'guide me with thy counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.' Faith returns and rises. The believer's poor portion now ends in everlasting glory.

Ashamed of his bitterness, he owns God still holds his hand, will 'guide me with thy counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.' Faith returns and rises. The believer's poor portion now ends in everlasting glory.

'Whom have I in heaven but thee?' he cries; God is 'the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.' He needs nothing beside God. When flesh and heart fail, God Himself is the soul's enduring treasure.

'Whom have I in heaven but thee?' he cries; God is 'the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.' He needs nothing beside God. When flesh and heart fail, God Himself is the soul's enduring treasure.
The far from God shall perish, 'but it is good for me to draw near to God.' He has found his answer. Nearness to God, not the wealth of the wicked, is the true and lasting good.
The far from God shall perish, 'but it is good for me to draw near to God.' He has found his answer. Nearness to God, not the wealth of the wicked, is the true and lasting good.
